title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Rosamund Darnley discusses her past with Hercule Poirot, reflecting on a childhood shaped by the English countryside and financial hardship. Despite her professional success as an artist and businesswoman, she expresses a sense of personal incompleteness, noting that she would prefer the companionship of a family over her material wealth.

Rosamund reveals that her preoccupation with the past was triggered by the arrival of Captain Kenneth Marshall and his family. She identifies Marshall as a childhood friend she has not seen in fifteen years, describing him as a good but foolish man who has a history of marrying problematic women. She specifically recounts his marriage to Eleanor Stewart, a woman she characterizes as a "gold digger" and a "man-eater" who exploits men. Rosamund details Eleanor's involvement in several scandals, including an acquittal after being accused of murdering her husband with arsenic, her role as a third party in a high-profile divorce, and her status as the beneficiary of Sir Roger Erskine's fortune.

Rosamund expresses concern that Eleanor is currently targeting Patrick Redfern. While she acknowledges that Patrick is a man devoted to his wife, Christine Redfern, she believes Christine is a reasonable person who will face a "rude awakening" when confronted by Eleanor's predatory nature.
